chunk_summary: This section discusses neurodevelopmental screening and psychosocial support in stunted children, emphasizing age-specific assessments, caregiver counseling, and referral pathways to address developmental vulnerabilities linked to chronic undernutrition.
learning_objectives: Understand the link between stunting and developmental vulnerabilities across different age groups.
Learn to implement age-appropriate neurodevelopmental screening tools and workflows.
Recognize the importance of caregiver mental health and psychosocial support in child development.
Familiarize with referral pathways like RBSK and DEIC for developmental interventions.
key_facts: Stunting is associated with developmental vulnerabilities, including height deficits and delayed neurodevelopment.

Screening should include age-specific domains: under 3 years (hearing, vision, motor skills), 3–5 years (language, behavior), school-age (attention, sleep), and adolescents (mood, body image, menstrual history).
The Trivandrum Developmental Screening Chart (TDSC) is a quick tool for children under 3 years.
The RBSK program facilitates screening and referral for children aged 0–18 years through District Early Intervention Centers (DEIC).
Caregiver stress and depressive symptoms should be addressed as determinants of child growth and development.
reference_quote: The RBSK program enables screening and referral across 0–18 years through District Early Intervention Centers (DEIC).
